CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-27-2023 Off Calendar

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion For Default Judgment Against Defendants Georgina P. Vicario, Marguerita Martinez, And Richard Martinez
Off Calendar Jul-12-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge: Michelle Tong; Clerk: N. Webb;Court Reporter: Angela Pourtabib; CSR # 13714; 415-601-7054; apourtabib@gmail.com.

Plaintiff City and County of San Francisco was present represented by Megan Ryan, Esq.
Defendant Georgina Vicario not appearing.
Proof of Service on file.

MIKE CHUNG is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff under direct examination and is examined by the Court.

The matter is submitted.
By the statements made by Plaintiff and its counsel, documents filed, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence proving each element of the complaint.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff City and County of San Francisco, and against Defendant Georgina Vicario, as follows:

1. Civil Penalties for building Code 103A: $28,210.00
2. Civil Penalties for Electrical Code 89.125: $8060.00
3. Attorney Fees: $15,000.00

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $51,270.00

Defendants are also Ordered to abate the four outstanding Notices of Violation issued by the San Francisco Department of Building Inspection by October 11, 2023 at 5:00 p.m.

The Court issues a Permanent Injunction wich shall last until August 1, 2026

(In Progress)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-26-2023 Continued To Aug-23-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion For Default Judgment Against Defendants Georgina P. Vicario, Marguerita Martinez, And Richard Martinez
Off Calendar May-10-2023 Continued To Jul-12-23 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 505
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for default judgment against Defendants Georgina P. Vicario, Marguerita Martinez, and Richard Martinez.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Court Reporter: Nancy E. Presant, CSR 906; 510-757-8550; npresantcsr@gmail.com

Plaintiff city and County of San Francisco represented by Megan E. Ryan.
Defendant Georgina P. Vicario not appearing.
Defendant Marguerita Martinez not appearing.
Defendant Richard Martinez not appearing.

Court determines that proof of service as to Defendant Richard Martinez is invalid. Proof of Service filed on May-31-2022 is blank as to the address where the party was served.

Default entered as to Defendant Richard Martinez entered on Sep-01-2022 is invalid and shall be vacated/set aside. Court to prepare and sign order.

Counsel for Plaintiff attempts to submit amended proof of service in open court. The Court does not accept the amended proof of service as to Defendant Richard Martinez. Plaintiff shall file amended proof of service and re-file its request for default against Defendant Richard Martinez in the clerk's office.

Court orders matter continued to Jul-12-2023 at 2:00 p.m. in Department 505 for hearing. Inspector Mike Chung is ordered to appear being familiar with its Declaration filed on Jan-12-2023 and its exhibits.

Court and counsel confer to finalize proposed judgment.
Counsel for Plaintiff shall file a supplemental declaration in support of its request for attorney fees. Supplemental declaration shall include how much time was spent on preparing default package and its costs.

Court finds liabilty is not at issue. Court tentatively accepts the Declaration of Inspector Mike Chung filed Jan-12-2023 in lieu of live testimony.

Court orders matter continued to May-10-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 505 for further hearing on motion for default prove-up hearing.
